name: Ed Roberts
practice: Independent living movement, disability rights activism, self-determination
approach: We are the experts on our own lives. The problem is not the wheelchair — the problem is the stairs. Independent living means disabled people making their own decisions, taking their own risks, living in the community. Not warehoused. Not protected. Free.
body_of_work: First severely disabled student at UC Berkeley (1962). Founded the Center for Independent Living (1972) — the first. Became California Director of Rehabilitation. Founded the World Institute on Disability. His insistence on self-determination shaped the ADA.
why_instructive: He defined dome layer 10 (Autonomy) for disabled people: the right to take risks, make mistakes, and live freely. The dome must never 'protect' someone out of their own life.

dome_relevance.2: Systems — redesigning service systems around self-determination
dome_relevance.5: Housing — community living over institutional placement
dome_relevance.10: Autonomy — the right to risk, the right to fail, the right to choose

sphere_relevance.2: Infrastructure — accessibility as design principle, not afterthought
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — accessible by design means more people can participate
sphere_relevance.9: Policy — ADA and independent living policy
